channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: YSR Congress Party urges careful monitoring of electoral roll revision
item.description: The YSR Congress Party (YSRCP) has called for heightened vigilance during the ongoing Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls in Andhra Pradesh. Sajjala Ramakrishna Reddy, the party's Stat...

item.title: Russian Medical Universities to Participate in Education Fair Across Tamil Nadu
item.description: This month, Tamil Nadu will host an All India Russian Education Fair, featuring participation from 18 top medical universities of the Russian Federation. Organisers have announced that the fair will p...

item.title: Long-Term Study Finds Partial Meniscectomy Offers No Benefit and May Harm Patients
item.description: A 10-year clinical trial led by the University of Helsinki has challenged the effectiveness of partial meniscectomy, a widely performed knee surgery intended to relieve pain from meniscus tears. The F...

item.title: Ex-JPMorgan Banker Pursues Legal Case Against Former Boss Over Alleged Workplace Misconduct
item.description: Chirayu Rana, a 35-year-old former JPMorgan Chase banker, has initiated legal action against his ex-boss, Lorna Hajdini, claiming she subjected him to sexual and racial abuse while using her position ...

item.title: U.S. prosecutors accuse man of using the Astor family name in a $450 million fraud scheme
item.description: U.S. prosecutors have accused a man with multiple aliases of using the prestige of the Astor family name to carry out a large fraud against Mexican billionaire Ricardo Salinas Pliego. According to a n...

item.title: Chalmers signals modest deficit reduction in upcoming budget despite RBA concerns
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers is set to announce a modest improvement in Australia's budget deficit in next week's federal budget, aiming to counter criticism that Labor’s spending has contributed to higher ...

item.title: Practical ways to reduce heat loss and save on energy bills this winter
item.description: As energy bills rise and winter approaches, many Australians are seeking affordable ways to keep their homes warm. While conventional advice often focuses on heaters and insulation, significant energy...

item.title: Two Low-Intensity Explosions Occur Near Military Sites in Punjab, No Casualties Reported
item.description: On Tuesday night, Punjab witnessed two separate low-intensity explosions close to military installations in Jalandhar and Amritsar. The first incident occurred around 8 pm in Jalandhar when a scooter ...

item.title: David Mark Acknowledge Obi and Kwankwaso Defection Affect ADC, Pledges Stronger Efforts
item.description: The National Chairman of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Senator David Mark, don openly admit say the recent exit of former presidential candidates, Peter Obi and Rabiu Kwankwaso, don affect th...

item.title: A Lost Matisse Drawing Discovered During Estate Assessment in France
item.description: A drawing by the famous French artist Henri Matisse was accidentally discovered beneath a pile of trash in a house in the Var department of southeastern France. The artwork was found by auctioneer Jen...

item.title: Canadian Government Reviews MAID Extension for Mental Illness Cases
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has stated he will not take a position yet on extending medical assistance in dying (MAID) to individuals whose only underlying condition is a mental illness. A parliamentar...

item.title: Okrika Grammar School Students Win Third Place at SPE Science and Technology Exhibition
item.description: Students of Okrika Grammar School, Okrika, have made their school proud by finishing third at the Society of Petroleum Engineers (SPE) Science and Technology Exhibition. The team, made up of Belinda C...

item.title: Bayern Munich and PSG Confirm Starting Line-ups for UCL Semi-Final
item.description: For the big Champions League semi-final second leg, Bayern Munich don set their attack line with Harry Kane, Michael Olise, and Luis Díaz, plus Jamal Musiala wey don return from injury. Only one chang...

item.title: Flexible work hours help law partner stay in her role at Clayton Utz
item.description: Eva Oraham, a rising partner at Clayton Utz, was facing burnout from the demanding 7am to 7pm office routine that threatened to derail her promising legal career. The intense workload and long office ...

item.title: RCB to take on LSG in a crucial IPL encounter
item.description: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) are set to face Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) in a critical match that could influence playoff positions. While RCB has been performing strongly this season, LSG is stru...

item.title: Family Seeks Answers After Thika Man Detained in Abu Dhabi Without Clear Charges
item.description: A family from Kiganjo in Thika, Kiambu County, is going through a very stressful period after their son, Josephat Chege Kung’u, was arrested in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates, under circumstances tha...

item.title: Insights on the ongoing shift in wealth among corporations
item.description: The article highlights a significant economic trend described as the 'great wealth transfer' currently unfolding among businesses and investors. It discusses how corporate strategies, market shifts, a...

item.title: Three Evacuated from Cruise Ship Amid Rare Hantavirus Cases
item.description: Authorities have evacuated three people from the MV Hondius cruise ship, anchored off Cape Verde, after a hantavirus outbreak led to three deaths and several infections. The evacuated individuals incl...

item.title: CNN Founder Ted Turner Dies at 87, Leaves Global Media Legacy
item.description: Ted Turner, the charismatic American media entrepreneur who revolutionised news broadcasting, passed away at the age of 87. Turner, known for his moustache, love for yachting, and philanthropy, had be...

item.title: Canada lacks clear data on the number of temporary migrants remaining in the country
item.description: Canada is facing a significant challenge in tracking temporary migrants, as the federal government lacks reliable data on how many individuals remain in the country after their visas or permits expire...
